explanation: The natural language input specifies that whenever the system is in training mode, the explanation provided should be detailed and contain all information. Since we only have an atomic proposition for 'detailed' (not for 'all information'), the best we can do is to ensure that 'detailed' holds whenever 'trainingMode' does. In LTL, this is usually captured by a global implication: globally, if trainingMode holds, then detailed must also hold. This is most directly represented by G(trainingMode -> detailed).

The given requirement states that when the system is in training mode, any explanation should be detailed and contain all information. Since we don't have an atomic proposition for 'containing all information', but we do have one for 'detailed', we will capture the requirement as: whenever 'trainingMode' holds, it is always the case that 'detailed' holds. In LTL, this is usually denoted as 'globally (if trainingMode then detailed)'. This ensures that at all times during training mode, the explanation is detailed. If 'trainingMode' is false, there is no requirement on 'detailed'.

The specification requires that whenever the system is in training mode, any explanation provided must be detailed (i.e., contain all information as implied by 'detailed'). This is captured by the formula: globally, if 'trainingMode' holds, then 'detailed' must also hold at that moment. Thus, every time the system is in training mode, the property enforces that the explanation is detailed. No other previous LTL property was provided for comparison.

The input specifies that whenever the system is in training mode, explanations should always be detailed and contain all information. Since there is no atomic proposition for 'contains all information', we focus on the 'detailed' aspect. To represent that, globally, whenever trainingMode holds, detailed must also hold at the same time. This is captured by the property G(trainingMode -> detailed), stating that at all times, if trainingMode is true, then detailed must be true as well. There were no previous LTL properties given for evaluation.

The requirement is that whenever the system is in training mode, any explanation provided should be both detailed and contain all information. Since we only have the atomic proposition 'detailed' to represent 'the explanation is detailed and contains all information', we interpret 'detailed' as capturing the essence of the desired property. Therefore, globally, whenever trainingMode holds, detailed must also hold: G (trainingMode -> detailed). This property prevents any occurrence of trainingMode where detailed is not also true, matching the intent of the natural language.

The requirement states that whenever the system is in training mode, the explanation should be detailed and contain all information. Given the atomic propositions, we can interpret this as: whenever trainingMode holds, detailed must also hold. This is a safety property that is adequately captured by the LTL formula 'G (trainingMode -> detailed)', which states that globally (at all times), if the system is in training mode, then the explanation is detailed. This formulation correctly models the requirement that in all training situations (i.e., whenever trainingMode is true), detailed must be true. No numerical comparisons are needed; it's solely about the implication between atomic propositions in all states.

The natural language states that when in training situations (indicated by 'trainingMode'), the explanation should be detailed and contain all information. The only available atomic proposition related to this is 'detailed'. To capture the requirement that explanations should always be detailed while in training mode, we use the release operator: (trainingMode V detailed). This formalizes that as long as 'trainingMode' holds, 'detailed' must also be true; when 'trainingMode' ceases to hold, the requirement is lifted.

The natural language requires that WHENEVER the system is in training mode, the explanation must be detailed (contain all information). This can be expressed in LTL by stating that globally (at all times), if 'trainingMode' holds, then 'detailed' must also hold. This ensures that at every point during any period when the system is in training mode, the explanation is always detailed. No numerical comparison is required here, and all information inclusion is enforced by the 'detailed' atomic proposition.

The natural language states that whenever the system is in 'training situations' (i.e., trainingMode is true), the explanation should be detailed and contain all information. Since 'contains all information' is not mappable to an atomic proposition but 'detailed' is, we use 'detailed' to capture the requirement. Using LTL, we require that globally, whenever 'trainingMode' holds, 'detailed' should also hold. This is expressed as G (trainingMode -> detailed).

This property ensures that at every moment, if the system is in training mode, then the explanation is detailed, which most closely matches the input natural language with the given propositions.

The natural language requires that when the system is in training situations (i.e., when 'trainingMode' is true), the explanation must be detailed and contain all information. Since there is an atomic proposition for 'detailed,' the property can be interpreted as: whenever 'trainingMode' holds, 'detailed' should also hold. In LTL, this is typically captured by the formula G(trainingMode -> detailed), which means: globally, whenever 'trainingMode' is true, 'detailed' is also true at the same moment. This ensures that in any state where trainingMode is active, detailed is also guaranteed. This captures the intent of the input natural language most precisely given the atomic propositions provided.
